title: pouteria retinervis
intro: Pouteria retinervis, also known as the "maple tree" or "satinwood," is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to Central and South America. The bark of Pouteria retinervis contains medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Pouteria retinervis include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Pouteria retinervis can be made by infusing the bark in hot water to create a tea or by mixing the bark with other herbs to create a salve or tincture.
